folio.label: 19r
folio.l: And lete hi~ , in this prison stille dwelle
And of Arcite , forth I wyl ©ow telle
¢ The Somer passeth , & the nygútes longe
Encresynge double wyse , the peynes stronge
Bothe of the louere , and of the prisoner
I ne wot which hath , the sorwefullest myster
For shortly to seyn , this Palamou~
îpetuelly , is dampned to prisou~
In cheynes and in feteres , to be ded
And Arcite is exiled , vp on his hed
For euäe more , ou©t of æat contre
Ne neuäe more , he shal his lady se
Now louyers I ask ©ow , this questiou~

note.notetxt: 489 A pointing hand is in the left margin.MP
489the pointing hand is in the same ink.ODR

folio.l: Who hath the werse , Arcite or Palamou~
That on may se his lady , day by day
But in prisou~ , mote he dwellen ay
That other where hi~ list , may ride or go
But sen his lady , shal he neuäe mo
Now demeth as ©ow list , ©e æat can
For I wyl telle ©ow forth , as I began
¢ Whan æat Arcite , to Thebes come was
Ful ofte a day , he swelte & seide allas
For sen my lalyladylaldy , shal I neuäe mo

note.notetxt: lady499The scribe originally wrote laly, the d is the result of an added loop to close the ascender of l.ODR

folio.l: And shortly to concluden , al his woo
So meche sorwe , had neuäe creature
That is or shal , while æe world may dure
His slepe his mete & drynk´ , is hi~ byraft´
That lene he wex & drye , as is a shaft´
His eyen holwe , and grysely to be holde
His hewe falwe , and pale , as asshen colde
And solitarie he was , and euäe allone
And walkyngö al æe nygút , makyngö his mone
And if he herd songö , or Instrument
Than wold he wepe , he myght nat stynt´
So feble eke were hise spiritã , and so lowe
And chaunged so , æ noman koude hi~ knowe
His speche ne his vois , though men yt herde
And in his gere , for al the world he ferde
Nou©t only lyke , the louyers maladye
Of hereos , but rather lyke Manye
